concept: Dig / Open the Earth / Work Downward into Soil
description: To dig: the planting hole, the well, the root from the ground. Opening the earth with hands or tools. Built on the sound of 'muila' (earth) — digging is earth-work by name.
sound_symbolism: 'mu' is the soil itself (the first syllable of 'muila'), 'ho' the breath of effort, 'li' the passage downward and through. Earth, work, way: the deed in order.
grammatical_notes: Transitive: 'lo mia thepalu muholi.' (We dig the garden.) For planting: 'mia lila thinoe muholi.' (I dig for the seeds.)

pillars.solarpunk-values: Soil turned by hand at garden scale — the future's agriculture looks like careful digging, not extraction.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Everything began with digging: the well, the cellar, the grave, the garden. The spade is the honest tool of settlement.
